item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, it's me, WizzBlizz, here to give you some crit!

Line By Line
Line By Line

I'll be doing line-by-line suggestions as I read through the article, so also consider this to be first impressions.

and converted to Site-090

Why not just Site-90? Seems like a weird name.

No personnel are allowed to enter SCP-4085 without authorization from the Director of Site-090.

Maybe specify the Site Director's name?

attempting to exit with items from SCP-4085 without purchasing,

Awkward wording. Just say 'stealing'.

a standard size grocery store

grocery stores come in many shapes and sizes, so I'm not sure what "standard" means here.

observed to have standard grocery store stock,

Again, different grocery stores have different items. Especially if you go to a different region or country.

The packaging of products such as chips or cookies appears normal, but when opened it can be observed that the contents are in the shape of human extremities such as fingers, noses, and tongues.

This is too many words to just say there are cookies in the shapes of fingers and tongues.

Analysis of the meat products found in SCP-4085 shows that they are genetically identical to human.

Just say the meat products are genetically identical to humans.

Analysis of packaged food products and produce inside SCP-4085 are normal,

normal how so? Haven't we established already that everything is shaped like humans? what food products are we actually talking about here? Does this include the meat products?

SCP-4085-A is the designation for the entities that exist inside SCP-4085

Again, you can simplify this like:

SCP-4085-A are [description of anomaly] that exist within SCP-4085

When observed from inside SCP-4085, they are humanoid in shape, but are 3 meters in height, have orange-tinted skin,

What does "humanoid in shape" mean? humanoid is a shape. Also, scrap the "but". Not sure why that is there.

If a subject causes a Σ-Window Shopper event

How do you cause one?

This will continue until the subject(s), who have not met the criteria, have been subdued.

What "criteria" are we talking about?

[See Investigation Log]

Just a personal preference, but this would fit better as a footnote.

were given Class B amnestic with false memory implementation.

Awkward phrasing. Try something like:

were administered Class B amnestics, including the construction of new memories.

You know, if you told people what to expect beforehand these expeditions would go a whole lot smoother.

Good point. Why would they not tell them about potentially dangerous anomalies they will be interacting with?

Oh my god… You guys see this right? It has a face. A human face!

They're getting a bit over-excited for a weirdly shaped fruit.

Please approach the entity and attempt to gather information from it.

The dialogue from Dr. Kellogg feels very awkward. When speaking to a D-Class during a mission, they should be clear and concise, rather than overly clinical. In this example, he hasn't even specified how the D-Class is supposed to gather information about it.

We make our fine products by infusing and capturing the finest of lost human souls!

The hook has arrived a bit too late to the party I think.

Well orange man

You can't repeat the same joke twice.

Sick fucking hell!

Very weird phrasing. Not even sure if he's saying something positive or negative.

I don't think these things are trying to help me anymore!

When were they trying to help them? Offering someone products isn't really "helping".

May you please describe what the entities are doing right now?

There is no reason for them to be this polite.

Ow! Fuck! Get off, get off!

This isn't the screaming in pain I would expect to hear from someone getting their leg torn apart.

what was happening to D-1213 could not be determined.

We know, you said it was malfunctioning.

D-rag is a term D-class personnel refer to other new D-class personnel.

This is obvious, no need to tell us.

General Thoughts
General Thoughts

- There are a lot of words you could take out of the description and still have this make just as much sense.
- The article is generally bloated. The Investigations went on for far too long and didn't really tell us anything we didn't already know.
- The dialogue felt very awkward. (I would try saying out loud to see how it feels)
- The idea itself has potential, but the article's bloat means it gets dragged out far too thin.
- I suggest cutting this article down massively and focusing on the core idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are a LOT of grammatical errors in this, especially in the logs.

item.title: Great idea but needs some "fleshing out"
item.content:encoded: A store with food made of human souls is a cool concept but some of the wording is a little nondescript or too casual to be in a formal document. I thought the tone could be a little less direct than 'normal grocery store stock' that have human features. I'm probably being a little over-critical though. It was still a great read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hello there! I've only ever posted one SCP, which failed miserably. I'm glad to see the number 4085 has gone to something that truly deserves it.

PoI-4590: [In a lower tone and volume than before] Nay. Dammit, and I thought customers were coming back. Well orange man, you better buy something real soon, unless you want to end up like that.
PoI-4590 is pointing to a bag of chips.

+1ed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hope you give it another read, I believed I fixed all the issues in the mechanical and little bit of the exposition in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept is nice, and the worldbuilding you're going for has potential, but there's too many mechanical errors for me to overlook. Interview SCP-4085 also feels like an exposition dump.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ditto. This is right on the border of upvote territory for me, so I really hope the author goes back and revises it a little.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Congrats on finally publishing! It's a good article, and has it's share of unique ideas. Some wordings could be improved, but otherwise a great piece. +1, can't wait for more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The imagery was really creepy but there are quite a few grammar mistakes and weird phrasings that id like to see addressed. Novote

item.title: :D
item.content:encoded:
gjgj

Anyway, I loved the horror aspect and the logs and transcripts. This is like 3008, but with another kinda feel to it. Loved this, definitely a +1. :D

item.title: Really Liked It!!
item.content:encoded: I found this to be really entertaining and a good unique concept, very well executed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really loved the whole thing. I was afraid it was a 3008 clone and was pleasantly surprised. +1 because im a sucker for D-class interactions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed reading this article. It's obvious you spent some time on it, and it wasn't wasted. My first impressions were that this was a rip off of SCP-3008, but I was glad when I read on and found it to not be a clone.
The tone was largely consistent, adn my only nitpick is that there should've been a bit more mystery to i.

item.title: Thanks to the people who helped.
item.content:encoded:
This is scp created by me and I would like to thank all my proofreaders.

Franklin Zhu

Manigeitora

DrLeibowitz

O5-■■: ZergRushLG

And to all the people irc chat and discord chat who gave me their thoughts.
